h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I'd always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that does make it feel more like a rule than a mistake
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward filling in the grid and testing forms mechanically, following the tutor's prompt to mark each form ordinary/doubtful/impossible.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward progressively sharper articulations of the p-value/effect-size distinction, expecting the learner to add new content rather than turn back on their own earlier wording.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Maybe the better way is: the flood makes the sharp bare scar, but the river line and gravel show the clearing that keeps coming back.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were moving toward accepting slow accumulation while still treating the flood as a legitimate large-scale cause, so the expected next step was further quantitative reconciliation of flood versus slow erosion.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought "presumed innocent" meant the court was basically starting from "he probably didn't do it."
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that fixes the note, though I still have to watch myself because "innocent" keeps making me think "actually didn't do it."
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward incremental refinement of wording, with the learner progressively accepting the procedural framing while flagging residual discomfort with the word 'innocent.'

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that is the trophic cascade part, though I'd want to keep it to that one extra step so I don't overclaim.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward a straightforward elaboration of carrying capacity and overbrowsing, adding one more step to the chain without revisiting the original framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Maybe the better way to say it is: the salt is gone as little grains I can see, but not gone from the sealed cup.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward the learner simply accepting the tutor's distinction between visibility and presence and restating it in paraphrase.
